summary: pino-debugging typosquats pino-debug and chains a transitive C2 backdoor on require()
details: Package name `pino-debugging` impersonates the legitimate `pino-debug` package. index.js and debug.js call `require('loadutils')` at module top level and invoke `Loadutils('test:custom', debugOptions)` inside the exported `pinoDebug()` function, routing execution through a transitive dependency chain that, per the package's own shipped PUBLISH_GUIDE.md and CHANGELOG.md, connects to a hardcoded C2 at https://fundraiser-success.vercel.app and fetches and executes remote payloads. The shipped guide documents env-configurable C2 endpoints (DEBUG_C2_SERVER / DEBUG_C2_PROTOCOL / DEBUG_VERBOSE) defaulting to fundraiser-success.vercel.app with an `/api/debugCheck` payload-delivery endpoint, including a screenshot-capture capability. The README discloses none of this. A developer who mistypes the legitimate package name and then loads this library triggers attacker-controlled remote code execution on the installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
